summary: Deprecated audit trail for the mixed network power-saving bundle. The official TCP/IP offload and MMCSS values are split into a child record; the opaque NetworkThrottlingIndex value remains tracked only here.

setting.name: Network Power and Multimedia Responsiveness Bundle
setting.casual_explanation: This tweak tries to change a few different settings that affect network offloads and multimedia scheduling responsiveness.
setting.why_people_change_it: People apply these settings when chasing lower latency for gaming or streaming and want fewer throttles or network-related bottlenecks.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: The bundle is not one single Windows setting. It mixes TCP/IP offload behavior with MMCSS scheduling behavior, and one of the values still lacks a primary Microsoft source in this review.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents DisableTaskOffload under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\TCPIP\Parameters and SystemResponsiveness under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile. The current app also writes NetworkThrottlingIndex in SystemProfile, but this review did not find a primary Microsoft source for that exact value name. The tweak title therefore overstates how cleanly these three writes map to one official 'disable network power saving' feature.

allowed_values.label: Task offload enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Microsoft documents that setting DisableTaskOffload to 0 enables task offloads.

allowed_values.label: Task offload disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Microsoft documents that setting DisableTaskOffload to 1 disables all task offloads from the TCP/IP transport.

allowed_values.label: App responsiveness value
allowed_values.meaning: Microsoft documents SystemResponsiveness as the percentage of CPU resources guaranteed to low-priority tasks. Values not divisible by 10 are rounded down to the nearest multiple of 10.

allowed_values.label: Primary-source mapping not yet confirmed
allowed_values.meaning: This review did not find a primary Microsoft source documenting the exact NetworkThrottlingIndex registry value.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app writes DisableTaskOffload = 0, NetworkThrottlingIndex = 0xFFFFFFFF, and SystemResponsiveness = 10. Only two of these values are backed by primary Microsoft documentation in this review, and the bundle mixes separate feature areas.

windows_defaults.label: Mixed Windows baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using TCP/IP offload and MMCSS features

states.rationale: Microsoft documents that 0 enables task offloads.
Microsoft documents how SystemResponsiveness behaves, but this record does not publish one stock cross-version default value.
Primary Microsoft documentation for this exact value name has not been confirmed in this review.

windows_defaults.notes: This bundle mixes separate Windows subsystems and is intentionally not published as one validated baseline.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that should keep stock network and MMCSS behavior
avoid_for: None until this mixed bundle is split and reviewed more cleanly

states.rationale: Keep the documented task-offload enabled baseline while the rest of the bundle remains under review.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to stay close to the stock Windows behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a fully reviewed latency-tuning preset right now.
tradeoffs: Windows keeps the stock blend of network and MMCSS behavior.
The current app bundle remains documented but not validated.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Using Registry Values to Enable and Disable Task Offloading
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents DisableTaskOffload under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\TCPIP\Parameters, with 1 disabling task offloads and 0 enabling them.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Multimedia Class Scheduler Service
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents SystemResponsiveness under the SystemProfile key and explains how the value is rounded and clamped.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableTaskOffload = 0, NetworkThrottlingIndex = 0xFFFFFFFF, and SystemResponsiveness = 10.

evidence.title: Repo power notes
evidence.summary: The repo tracks these values as power and network tuning observations, but this review did not elevate NetworkThrottlingIndex to a primary-source-backed Windows control.

decision.why: This tweak mixes separate control surfaces and one of the written values still lacks a primary Microsoft source in this review, so the bundle should stay in review rather than in the validated set.
blocking_issues: The bundle mixes TCP/IP offload behavior with MMCSS scheduling behavior.
NetworkThrottlingIndex has not been confirmed from a primary Microsoft source in this review.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Microsoft Learn: Multimedia Class Scheduler Service: Microsoft documents SystemResponsiveness under the SystemProfile key and explains how the value is rounded and clamped.
validation_proof.notes: Backfilled from evidence_id ms-mmcss (official-doc); deprecated audit trail.
